compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Diagnosis + research recorded.

**Root cause (firing gap):** `PlanContext.level` is derived from `ticket_type` (det_floor.py:117);
`registry.applies()` gates on that type-derived `level`. T5c security overlay is `levels:["task"]`,
so `applies(T5c, level="epic")` → False. The Gerrit epic d251 (container) was never security-reviewed.
Sole `applies()` call site: orchestrator.route_criteria (orchestrator.py:284). Full type/level surface
mapped: applies(), is_mechanical_leaf (`ticket_type=="task"`), size_factor (`"epic"`), _ticket_size
(`ctx.level=="epic"`), PlanContext.level, all 32 criteria_routing.json applies_at blocks.

**Content gap:** 3/3 live T5c runs against d251 flag adjacent issues (unencrypted token volume,
token-in-logs) but never the missing endpoint auth — T5c's anti-FP text brands "declared access
level"/"endpoint authn" as canonical false positives (calibrated for rebar-the-local-tool).

**Research (needle-threading, cited):** the industry gate for "auth in scope?" is trust-boundary
crossing / reachability by an untrusted actor (STRIDE Spoofing↔authn; OWASP ASVS 1.4.1 enforcement
points; Semgrep/Anthropic reachability > category). "Undeclared auth posture = defect" is codified:
deny-by-default (OWASP Proactive Controls C5) + ASVS 1.1.4 ("justification of all trust boundaries")
→ require an explicit auth decision: a mechanism OR a justified no-auth (e.g. loopback-only/behind
gateway). Positive-pass carve-out for pure libraries / in-process modules / single-user local CLIs.
Non-security angle to test: T10 (overlay-infra) has NO access-control bullet and only fires on infra
intent (naturally FP-resistant); E2.constraints_stated already references "an API's auth".

**Experiment matrix:** POS=d251 (want FLAG), NEG-hard=8a1c (local "gate security" signing, want
NO-FLAG), NEG-cert=4702 (review-certification attestations, want NO-FLAG). Baseline running.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Refactor (routing axis) — DONE + green
Replaced ticket-type scrutiny with container/leaf:
- `registry.applies()` drops the type-derived `level`; keys on has_children (container/leaf `scope`).
- `is_mechanical_leaf(plan, *, has_children)` — leaf, not `ticket_type=="task"`.
- `size_factor`/`_ticket_size` drop the `"epic"` literal → `has_children`.
- `PlanContext.level` (type→level map) removed (now unused).
- `det_floor` P9 file-impact: `not ctx.children` (leaf), dropping the `("task","story","bug")` type set — a childless epic (leaf-shaped) now correctly gets the check.
- all 32 `criteria_routing.json` applies_at → `scope`: G3/G4=container; code-grounding/impl-detail (E4/A1/G1G2/E6/T5a/T5b/E5/T5d/T6/T9)=leaf; everything else=both. **T5c security is no longer altitude-gated (both)** — the fix.
- validator rejects legacy `levels`/`container_only` with a migration hint + validates `scope`.
- E5 rubric prose updated (epic-exclusion → leaf-only). New tests: container/leaf applies, mechanical-leaf, legacy-key rejection, scope validation. 228 plan-review tests pass; `make lint` clean; routing parity gate OK.

## Content fix (Layer 2) — experiments
Harness = Pass-1 finder over each candidate vs the epic (POS=d251) + local negatives (8a1c, 4702).
- **Baseline T5c** on d251 (4 samples): never flags the missing Gerrit human/admin auth (flags
  encryption-at-rest + token-in-logs, or nothing). Negatives: no auth FP. ✓ "old did not flag".
- **C1 (T5c trust-boundary calibration)**: negatives clean (no FP), but POS still misses the auth
  absence — crowded out by the louder encryption/token findings.
- **C2 (T10 + endpoint_access_contract bullet)**: T10 is FP-safe (0 findings on local 8a1c — doesn't
  fire on non-infra), but first-draft bullet also lost the slot to encryption.
- Root difficulty: the gap is an ABSENCE in a plan dense with OTHER auth (deploy keys, webhook
  tokens, SSM), read as "auth handled"; louder findings win the finder's slots.
- **C3 (sharp T10 bullet)** under test: forces per-service enumeration of stood-up services + demands
  HUMAN/ADMIN auth explicitly, and rules that service-to-service creds do NOT satisfy it. T10 home
  chosen (infra-scoped ⇒ inherently FP-safe on local work; matches the "endpoint contract
  completeness" framing). Deterministic keyword checks rejected for plan PROSE (over-fire; belong to
  the code-review gate on the *.tf/gerrit.config diff).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Routing fix proven end-to-end (no LLM, deterministic)
route_criteria over the real assembled context:
- Container epic d251 (has_children=True): **T5c security NOW FIRES** (was filtered out before — the bug), G3 container-criterion fires, E4 leaf-criterion does not. 22 criteria fire.
- Childless epic 8a1c (TYPE epic, but 0 children = LEAF): T5c fires, G3 does NOT fire, **E4 leaf code-grounding DOES fire** — 30 criteria. Proves scrutiny follows container/leaf, not ticket type (a type=epic ticket gets LEAF criteria when childless; old type-logic would have withheld them).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Content fix SHIPPED + validated
**T10 infra overlay gains one `endpoint_access_contract` bullet** (rubric + checklist): every
network-reachable service a plan STANDS UP must state human/admin auth (named mechanism OR
justified no-auth); machine creds (deploy keys/tokens/SSM) do NOT satisfy it. Grounded in
deny-by-default (OWASP C5) + trust-boundary reachability (STRIDE/ASVS 1.4.1) + ASVS 1.1.4.
T10 chosen because it fires ONLY on infra intent ⇒ inherently FP-safe on local work.

### Validation on the Gerrit epic d251 (the specific ask)
Harness = Pass-1 finder, REAL shipped criterion (no override):
- **OLD** (T5c, type-gated to task): never ran on the epic (applies(T5c,epic)=False); forced to run,
  4 samples flagged only encryption/token — the missing Gerrit auth was NOT flagged. ✓ (1) old did not flag.
- **NEW** (T10 endpoint_access_contract): flagged the missing human/admin auth in **6/6** runs,
  correctly naming auth.type=DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT + the 0.0.0.0/0 exposure. ✓ (2) new flags.
- **Negatives** (local, no-network, security-FLAVORED): 8a1c (gate-signing) 0 findings; 4702
  (attestations) 0 findings — **0/4 false positives**.

591 unit tests pass; routing parity gate OK; make lint clean; prompt-index in sync.

compiled_state.description:
# Plan-review proportionate-scrutiny keys on ticket TYPE, not container/leaf — security overlay never fires on container epics

## Summary
Plan-review's proportionate-scrutiny routing decides which criteria run using ticket **type**
(epic/story/task), not the container(has_children)/leaf distinction. `PlanContext.level` is
literally derived from `ticket_type` (`det_floor.py:117`). Because the security overlay **T5c**
is routed `applies_at.levels: ["task"]`, it is filtered out for any `epic`/`story` — including
**container epics that stand up real infrastructure**. This let the AWS-hosted Gerrit epic
(`d251`) pass plan-review with **no authentication contract specified** for an internet-hosted
service (the `DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` default; now tracked as bug `f2d6`/WS8).

## Reproduction Steps
1. `applies(registry.by_id()["T5c"], level="epic")` → returns **False** (confirmed).
2. The Gerrit work `d251` is a container epic (children S1–S7) describing a networked service
   ("Gerrit HTTP 8080 + SSH 29418; reverse proxy on 443") with no user/admin auth model.
3. Its plan-review therefore never ran the security overlay against the epic's contents.
4. (Content layer) Even when T5c is forced to run against the epic, 3/3 live runs flag adjacent
   issues (unencrypted token volume, token-in-logs) but never the missing endpoint auth — the
   rubric's anti-false-positive text brands "declared access level"/"endpoint authn" as canonical
   false positives (calibrated for rebar-the-local-tool), suppressing the needed finding.

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** Proportionate scrutiny keys on container vs leaf; a criterion representing a
  cross-cutting risk surface (security) runs wherever that surface is described — container or
  leaf. A container epic that stands up a networked service is reviewed for its auth contract.
- **Actual:** Scrutiny keys on ticket type; T5c is `task`-only; container epics standing up
  infrastructure are never security-reviewed at the epic altitude, and an under-specified auth
  contract passes.

## Scope
- IN: the proportionate-scrutiny **routing axis** only — `registry.applies()` (drop the
  type-derived `level`; key on has_children), `is_mechanical_leaf`, `size_factor`, `_ticket_size`,
  `PlanContext.level`, the sole `applies()` call site (`orchestrator.route_criteria`), and all 32
  criteria's `applies_at` (`levels`/`container_only` → a container/leaf `scope`). Fix the
  mis-scoped security overlay so it fires on containers. Plus a concise, generic content fix so the
  gate detects an under-specified endpoint auth contract (networked service, needs identity, no
  auth plan) WITHOUT false-positiving local/no-network tools (rebar's own review certification).
- OUT: the Gerrit auth remediation itself (`f2d6`/WS8); `det_floor._clarity_score` per-type
  headings and the bug/session_log gate exemption (separate type axes, documented follow-ups).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `registry.applies()` no longer takes/uses a type-derived `level`; proportionate scrutiny is
      decided by container(has_children)/leaf. `PlanContext.level` and its type→level mapping are
      removed (or no longer used by any routing path).
- [ ] `is_mechanical_leaf`, `size_factor`, `_ticket_size` no longer key on the `"task"`/`"epic"`
      type strings; they key on container/leaf (has_children).
- [ ] All 32 `applies_at` blocks in `criteria_routing.json` express scope as container/leaf, not
      epic/story/task levels; each criterion's scope is re-mapped faithfully (child-coverage
      criteria G3/G4 → container; code-grounding/impl-detail → leaf; cross-cutting → both). The
      security overlay T5c is no longer altitude-gated away from containers.
- [ ] A concise, generic criterion change (security and/or a non-security endpoint-contract
      completeness check, chosen by experiment) makes the gate flag an under-specified auth
      contract for a networked service, with a documented negative-control run proving no false
      positive on local/no-auth work.
- [ ] Validation on `d251`: the OLD criteria did not flag the missing Gerrit auth; the NEW criteria
      do. Negative controls (`8a1c`, `4702`) do not regress to false positives.
- [ ] `validate_packaged_routing()` passes; existing plan-review tests updated to the new
      container/leaf routing; new test(s) covering container-scoped security + the endpoint-contract
      check; `make lint` clean.

file_impact.reason: applies() container/leaf scope; is_mechanical_leaf/size_factor; scope validation
all 32 applies_at -> scope; T10 endpoint_access_contract bullet

compiled_state.title: Plan-review scrutiny keyed on ticket type not container/leaf — security overlay skips container epics
